programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The heir to a Miami sugar fortune miraculously survives a shooting in which two other people are killed - but his narrow escape seems too good to be true and arouses the team's suspicions. Meanwhile, Calleigh's father asks her to investigate the case of a woman who stabbed her boyfriend to death with a screwdriver. Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An innocent drink at a bikini bar takes a turn for the worse when cyanide-laced water claims the lives of an exotic dancer and a navy lieutenant, and leaves a pilot fighting for his life. It's up to the team to determine whether it was an isolated incident - or a practice run for a large-scale poisoning. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star as the agents

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Dr Richard Kimble, wrongfully convicted and sentenced to death for the murder of his wife, escapes from custody and becomes a fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A distraught husband is accused of killing his scheming wife with a shot from a high-powered rifle fired from a rooftop

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker helps a man come to terms with his past in order to carry on his father's legacy. Wilma Knight makes a cameo appearance alongside son Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: The body of a Mexican cartel boss is snatched from a morgue in Tijuana, so Kensi and Deeks head to the scene of the crime, while Callen and Sam stay behind to ponder the possibility they have a mole in their ranks. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell, LL Cool J, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.desc: Comic Relief special, co-written by Notting Hill and Love Actually creator Richard Curtis. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part story filmed in London and Germany in collaboration with the cast and crew of drama series SOKO: Leipzig. Jack and his team track the kidnap gang to Germany and help arrange a ransom pick-up at Leipzig railway station, but the operation proves to be a set-up leading nowhere. The case takes a violent turn, prompting an unexpected confession from the kidnapped girl's stepfather (Max Herbrechter) and a desperate race to reach the hostages before it is too late

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl and Carlito's relationship leaves the Knight family reeling, the tension between Hugh and Penny endangers the new cardiac clinic, and Matt reconnects with a high school crush

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay

Martin Roberts, meanwhile, is surprised by the extra benefits that come with a pair of old garages in South London

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A wedding video cracks the case of a teenager's abduction and an expired pilot's licence exposes a fraudster flying under the radar. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Bear are at loggerheads, while Karen hopes for a relaxing day out with Rob. Ruhma finds a mother has disturbing symptoms following the birth of her baby

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne and his experts restore a Lloyd Loom-style chair, a pair of bookcases, and a pine table with matching chairs recovered from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in north Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart and Rainie head to the police station about Abi, while Karen and Tiffany accompany Bernie to her first weight-loss class. Mick is running late for Linda's scan, only for events to conspire against him - will he make it there on time? Lily is angry when Ruby betrays her trust, and Rocky tries his luck with Kathy once again

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick explains his absence to Linda and the couple discuss what's best for them and the baby. After telling Martin the bad news from the doctor, Ruby spends the evening with Lily and the pair begin to bond. Rocky steps in to help Kathy with the cafe, persuading her to finally take his number, and Tiffany feels left out as Keegan learns he has been shortlisted for the street food competition

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian finds it hard to keep his cool when Fletch blames him for Evie's disappearance. Sacha and Dom prepare to be interviewed for the clinical lead job. Will Sacha's experience win against Dom's ruthless ambition? Josh is determined to crack a mystery case, hoping to impress Ange and rekindle their relationship

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal-worker Dominic Chinea has a treat in store when he works on a rare bicycle sidecar that has not been used in decades, while furniture restorer Will Kirk needs all his skills when he is presented with a box of bits that used to be a Victorian sewing table, crushed to pieces in an accident. Can he piece this fragile 3D jigsaw back together? Percussion expert Pete Woods takes receipt of a military drum that saw frontline action in both world wars and horologist Steve Fletcher tries to get an art deco clock chiming again

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the opening matches in Group F - Hungary v Portugal at Puskas Arena in Budapest and France v Germany at Fußball Arena München. The match in Munich brought together the two most recent World Cup winners, and was also a rematch of their Euro 2016 semi-final, which the French won 2-0 in Marseille courtesy of two Antoine Griezmann goals. Germany coach Joachim Low will leave his post this summer after 15 years in charge - he oversaw the World Cup win in 2014 but couldn't prevent his side from suffering a shock early exit in 2018. Former England internationals Alex Scott and Rob Green provide analysis

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a European Championship-related countdown

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma's relationship with Romero takes a significant step, but Norman may not react well to the news. Meanwhile, Dylan tries to rid himself of old business. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman embarks on an impromptu adventure with a new friend, and while he's gone, Norma is given a taste of happiness at the White Pine Bay Festival. Romero faces an awkward situation and Dylan is caught between two families. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Germany. A chance to see both sides' opening Group F fixture, which took place at Fußball Arena München. With commentary from Jonathan Pearce

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits houses in Liverpool and Carlisle, and Dion Dublin is off to investigate a plot of land in Bury

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville ask who is responsible when valuables are stolen from a coach. Plus, the bride and groom left waiting at the airport

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A van's everyday satnav puts a pair of prolific animal thieves behind bars. An anonymous letter holds the key to cracking the identity of a blackmailer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at the Mona Showground on Anglesey, while host Anita Manning visits a jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Russia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group B fixture, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only met on four previous occasions, most recently in June 2009, when Russia recorded a straightforward 3-0 victory in a World Cup qualifier in Helsinki. Both sides will be delighted if they can secure three points here in their efforts to usurp group favourites Belgium and former winners Denmark, although the fact that three nations will progress from four of the six groups certainly improves their chances of reaching the last 16. Analysis is provided by Alex Scott and Micah Richards, while commentary comes from Robyn Cowen and Martin Keown

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the Group A encounter, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Current Wales manager Robert Page played in that match, and the former Watford defender will be hoping his back line will be more robust in this game. Page will also view this fixture as crucial to his side's chances of qualifying for the knockout stages once again, with the daunting prospect of a match against Italy in Rome still to come on Sunday. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. Callum feels helpless about Fitzy, so Whitney promises to help him, Suki gets on the wrong side of Vi, and Nancy and Frankie meddle in Shirley's love life. Jean has some advice for Ruby, who feels like giving up

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. Ben is suspicious when he spots Callum and Whitney getting into a cab together, Tiffany tries to cover her mistake and Rocky pulls out the stops to apologise to Sonia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make a little girl's bridesmaid dress, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodice and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - Turkey v Wales and Italy v Switzerland in Group A, and Finland v Russia in Group B. Wales made the semi-finals of Euro 2016 despite losing their second group fixture to England. The Welsh had not beaten Turkey since 1981, while the last meeting - a World Cup qualifier in 1997 - resulted in a 6-4 win for the Turks in Istanbul

programme.title: Roberto Martinez: Whistle to Whistle
programme.desc: Cameras follow the Belgium national team manager Roberto Martinez as he prepares for Euro 2020. The documentary provides behind-the-scenes access to Martinez as he carries out his dual role as head coach and technical director of the number one ranked team in the world

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales. A chance to see the second Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Commentary from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer explore the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire. Phil has his eye on a few quality antique staples, as well as one hefty horticultural item. Meanwhile, Izzie finds herself in what could well be her perfect shop, containing lots and lots of her favourite sparkly pieces. A magnificent Elizabethan Country House provides the backdrop for their final face-off

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rajeena and Hardeep have moved to their dream house in Great Glen boasting incredible views. However, its garden is an uninspiring blank canvas with an ugly bank of grass that obscures their vista rather than the traditional garden with a modern twist that they would prefer. The bank needs sorting and they want an entertaining area that capitalises on their view across the fields. A water feature and privacy is also important to the pair. Brothers David and Harry Rich compete with Charlie Dimmock to have their designs accepted to transform the patch on a £3,500 budget

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day one of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring matches from the first round. This tournament, along with Wimbledon, was cancelled last year due to the Covid-19 pandemic, meaning the players will be all the more eager to impress and adapt to their grass-court surroundings, and the seeded players include stars such as Matteo Berrettini, Diego Schwartzman, Milos Raonic and Stan Wawrinka

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling on to the capital city of Madrid, Michael visits the 1930s art deco Telefonica Building and discovers what happened there during the civil war

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from the fifth and final day of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Will Foster compete to remodel a couple's 1950s home in Belfast, with Lei Ying dreaming of a perfect kitchen, while her husband David hopes for a cosy home and a safe route to the garden. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Peter Taylor: Ireland After Partition
programme.desc: In a personal journey to mark the centenary of the partition of Ireland in 1921, award-winning journalist and documentary-maker Peter Taylor revisits the films he has made over the past 50 years to reflect on the issue of a united Ireland. He reveals how his own understanding of it has evolved, from the early days when he took a Hull bus driver and his wife to Belfast, and a group of Catholic and Protestant children to Wales, through to his interviews with MI6 officers and others involved in the secret talks between the British government and the IRA

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Dawn and Mick are hosting their annual Last Night of the Proms party as the perfect antidote to post-Brexit Britain, with everyone expected to wave their Union Jacks and sing along to Land of Hope and Glory, But Dawn's sister Penny has seen a mysterious stranger lurking around the neighbourhood and her husband Brian isn't feeling particularly patriotic and by the time the fat lady sings everyone's lives will be changed for ever. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Sarah Parish and Julian Glover

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: Single mother and immigrant Sonia works in sheltered accommodation, so when a resident makes an official complaint against her, she must continue to face everyone without bias. Comedy, starring Maria Popistasu

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Just Call Me Martina
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents a profile of former tennis star Martina Navratilova, documenting her achievements on the court in a professional career that spanned more than 30 years and saw her win Grand Slam titles in four different decades. Martina also goes back to her home town of Revnice in the Czech Republic, and allows the cameras to film her wedding in New York and the celebrations in Miami. Featuring contributions by Chris Ever, Elton John, Stephen Fry, Billie Jean King, Novak Djokovic, Rafael Nadal, Pam Shriver and Rod Laver

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: The Black American Fight for Freedom
programme.desc: Fifty years on from the promise of equality and the Civil Rights Act, this documentary examines moments where America had the chance to become more equal and why that didn't happen. Through interviews with those who were at the forefront of the struggle for a fair system in education, housing and criminal justice, the film reveals the key court cases, Supreme Court rulings and laws that failed to bridge the gap

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the third lockdown begins to lift in the middle of April 2021, emergency cases inevitably begin to rise, which puts an incredible amount of pressure of bed availability and staff across the country. At Coventry's University Hospital, Critical Care is at full capacity, and two neurosurgeons must find a solution to treat both of their patients with only one bed available

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion looks back at a selection of childhood antiques and collectibles bought by past contestants. Anita Manning learns about the childhood game of marbles, while Natasha Raskin Sharp chats to expert Tim Weeks about collecting toys

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Hannah, Elayne, Rupesh and Greg vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill introduce a special Gardeners' World from Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in West Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millennium Seed Bank

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Leeds Rhinos, Leigh Centurions v Catalans Dragons and Huddersfield Giants v Wigan Warriors

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day two of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring matches from the first round. Wild card Feliciano Lopez of Spain was the champion when this tournament was last staged in 2019, but was made to work hard for a 6-7, 6-3, 6-4 victory over Hungary's Marton Fucsovics in round one. One player looking to take Lopez's crown is Andy Murray, who missed the French open to focus on the grass season and is looking to put an injury-ravaged few years behind him

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael continues his journey through Spain with his 1930s Bradshaw's guide. At the Museo Reina Sofia, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th-century's most shocking works of art. Later in the northern town of Huesca, Michael meets the son of novelist George Orwell

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson begins with her take on a classic yoghurt pot cake, which was inspired by a scribbled note she found in an old Italian kitchen. The food writer's niece and nephew are recruited to make - and enjoy - a sausage meatball supper, while a girls' night in sees her rustling up squid and prawns with marjoram and chilli. The show ends with Nigella's 'tiramisini' - a lighter, quicker version of a traditional tiramisu

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Ranvir Singh, Micky Flanagan, Sophie Willan and Reginald D Hunter to discuss Shuggie Bain by Douglas Stuart, and The Frequency of Us by Keith Stuart, and they also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers in Pembrokeshire, including brothers Hal, Will and Ben, race to the scene when a plane makes an emergency crash landing on one of Wales' busiest beaches. In Jersey, the St Catherine crew are pushed to the brink when they search for a man who has gone missing at sea in the middle of a fierce winter storm. On the Wirral, the crew of Hoylake launch their specialist rescue hovercraft and race the incoming tide to try and save a horse, which has become stuck fast in the mud and at risk from drowning

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust assess the aftermath of the pandemic and its long-term impact, and with the plastic surgery department finally able to start elective operations again, difficult decisions must be made over the investment in robotic surgery

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Jonathan Ross, Gabby Logan, Mark Wright, Anita Rani and Rachel Parris play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: The Statue Wars: One Summer in Bristol
programme.desc: On Sunday, June 7, 2020, protestors marching to support the Black Lives Matter movement tore down the statue of slave trader Edward Colston and threw it in Bristol's harbour. Caught in the eye of this storm was Bristol's mayor Marvin Rees, the first directly elected mayor of Black African heritage of a major European city. Born and bred in Bristol and himself a descendant of enslaved people, how would he hold the city together in the face of rising tensions that threaten to explode into violent confrontation?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Former wheelchair racer Richie Powell investigates the sport's classification system, asking whether something designed to make competition between athletes with different disabilities fair is flawed, easily manipulated and lacking credibility

programme.title: Joey Essex: Grief and Me
programme.desc: The reality TV star opens up about the death of his mother, who took her own life when he was 10 years old. With the help of clinical psychologist Dr Stephen Blumenthal, Joey sets out to finally face the impact his mum's death has had on him. Until now he's only opened up about his childhood with his family, but sister Frankie and cousin Chloe recognise that he's never truly processed his feelings or fully faced his bereavement

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins the East Yorkshire bus company in Hull, getting behind the wheel of a double decker, making sure the vehicles go out on time and helping to keep them in perfect working order

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tries to spot one of the most photogenic birds in the UK - the black-throated diver. Plus, live updates from Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan in Norfolk - is there any sign of the elusive turtle dove?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair, and finds out about a distinctive style of wood carving. Experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine assist the teams

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Liam, Sam, Sarah and Kiya

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis makes a temporary comeback in the Den, standing in for Peter Jones who is self-isolating. A father-and-daughter duo demonstrate their airbag safety equipment, and a self-balancing serving tray is put through its paces by a hands-on Theo. The Dragons are bewildered by a multitudinous mix of merchandise on offer by a planet-saving entrepreneur, and finally an Irish couple pitch their anti-roll changing mat for babies

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day three of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the opening second-round singles matches. The first four matches will be expected to take place today, with the concluding four tomorrow, and the players in action will hope to follow in the footsteps of some unlikely victors at this stage in 2019. Unseeded players Diego Schwartzman and Gilles Simon, qualifier Nicolas Mahut, and wildcard entrant Feliciano Lopez all managed to progress to the last eight, with Lopez benefiting from a walkover victory when Juan Martin del Potro had to withdraw without taking to the court

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims. Beginning his journey in historic Orleans, Michael follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, before taking a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car. Finally in Versailles, Michael visits the opulent palace and neighbouring Trianon Palace hotel

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. Here, Fred heads to Marrakesh in the company of TV chef Andi Oliver, whose love of soul food has led to an obsession with the city's rich culinary heritage. The maitre d' reveals he has been to Marrakesh before but has yet to have a truly memorable meal. Can Andi change his mind as they are served lamb and couscous at a legendary family restaurant, chicken tagine for under £10, and breakfast on a spectacular roof terrace?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Bite-sized edition of the documentary, which sees Giles Coren and Monica Galetti working alongside the staff of Marina Bay Sands hotel in Singapore

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust assess the aftermath of the pandemic and its long-term impact, and with the plastic surgery department finally able to start elective operations again, difficult decisions must be made over the investment in robotic surgery

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a tour of Spring's finest in the Royal Botanic Gardens, Edinburgh, showcasing riveting rhododendrons and majestic meconopsis. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray get stuck into Beechgrove's bog and pond areas, while in sunny Joppa, George Anderson continues to show how to garden in small spaces with his summer bedding in pots

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: How age-blind casting, such as Ian McKellen in a new production of Hamlet, can change the way we perceive creative work. Lionel Shriver, whose new novel is about a married medical couple contemplating ending their lives in old age, discusses why her characters change their minds as they get older. And Mary Beard finds out why former Coronation Street star Julie Hesmondhalgh has written a play she can perform for the rest of her life

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism. In Cambridge, he revisits his former university to hear about a treacherous time in its past

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan report live from Norfolk, and Iolo Williams heads up into the Scottish Highlands to spot some flamboyant avian show-offs. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Work on the derelict miller's cottage appears to be progressing, but Griff Rhys Jones meets opposition from the locals over his plans to renovate the mill. Plus, the comedian takes on another unusual restoration project

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a winter scene with a touch of warmth and golden sunlight

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and expert Philip Mould explore the history of three paintings bequeathed to the National Museum of Wales when their owner died in 1951. It was always believed the pieces were by landscape artist JMW Turner, but only months after the museum took ownership, experts branded them fakes and unfit to hang on the gallery walls. Now Philip sets out to prove their authenticity once and for all

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny presents coverage of the second round of the contest from St David's Hall, with singers from Denmark, South Korea, South Africa and the home nation of Wales competing for a place in the final. Performing music by Mozart, Verdi, Mendelssohn and Handel, the entrants will be accompanied by the BBC National Orchestra of Wales, conducted by Andrew Litton

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: In this series, Eamonn McCabe traces the story of British photography, from the rapid innovation of Roger Fenton in the 1800s to the satirical eye of Martin Parr in the 21st century. In the third and final episode, he focuses on the explosion of colour images in the late 1950s, to the ongoing impact of the digital revolution

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a winter scene with a touch of warmth and golden sunlight

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Work on the derelict miller's cottage appears to be progressing, but Griff Rhys Jones meets opposition from the locals over his plans to renovate the mill. Plus, the comedian takes on another unusual restoration project

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and expert Philip Mould explore the history of three paintings bequeathed to the National Museum of Wales when their owner died in 1951. It was always believed the pieces were by landscape artist JMW Turner, but only months after the museum took ownership, experts branded them fakes and unfit to hang on the gallery walls. Now Philip sets out to prove their authenticity once and for all

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Restoration work on the miller's cottage continues, but across the lane building works at the mill grind to halt as the planning authorities investigate local objections to Griff Rhys Jones's plans. Meanwhile, the comedian makes plans for a music festival

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross employs some of his well-known oval magic to paint a cabin scene situated in a serene, soft, rolling pasture

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim promises to ensure a city farm project stays open - but devious Sir Humphrey uses his pledge as an excuse to get an additional nine floors added to the design of a proposed skyscraper. Comedy, guest starring Sue Lawley, with Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey takes a holiday, but frets about leaving her beloved Grantleigh Manor in DeVere's hands. Penelope Keith stars

programme.title: Reporting History: Mandela and a New South Africa
programme.desc: In a programme first shown on the 25th anniversary of Nelson Mandela's election as South Africa president, Fergal Keane goes back to examine his reports and considers why history did not turn out how he expected

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Young singers from the USA, Russia, South Korea and Austria perform music by Wagner, Purcell, Mozart and Handel. Hosted by Petroc Trelawny, with counter-tenor Iestyn Davies giving his thoughts, as well as judges Neal Davies, Roberta Alexander and Aidan Lang

programme.title: South Africa in Pictures
programme.desc: British photographer Rankin, whose subjects have included Britney Spears, Kylie Minogue, Damien Hirst and the Queen, turns his focus on South Africa. He explores the country's rich history on film, and discovers the role the art has played in recording the story of its people. Includes contributions by war reporters the Bang Bang Club and photographers David Goldblatt and Alf Kumalo

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary using archive performance footage and interviews to explore the life of Miriam Makeba, the Johannesburg-born singer and civil rights activist who was forced into exile in 1960 for opposing the apartheid regime. Miriam became one of the first South African musicians to make a name for herself on the international scene, and she used her music to deliver messages against racism and poverty

Historical comedy sketches, starring Mark Gatiss, Reece Shearsmith and Sarah Hadland

programme.title: Snaps
programme.desc: Would you turn your back on your religion just so you could fit in? 14 year-old Jag is a care-free, easy-going Sikh boy who loves nothing more than playing football with his mates, but he has finally lost his patience with his classmates after the new kid at school asks if he is wearing a handkerchief around his topknot. Jag feels like he spends his life answering stupid questions about his hair and has had enough. Is this something he is going to have to face all his life? And why didn't his friends stick up for him? Would life be simpler if he just cut his hair? After all his brother Kully did just that, and life seems easy for him now...but it's not quite that simple for Jag. In this short coming-of age-film, we see Jag make one of the toughest decisions of his life to date. Does he honour his Sikh family values and traditions and start to wear a turban, or will he cut off his hair to fit in with his mates?

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘slippers’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. This episode is all about slippers – green slippers, blue slippers, little slippers and big slippers. We see prickly slippers and pom pom slippers, monster slippers and fairy slippers! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug wants to break a world record. Dog suggests they go to the North Pole so that Pug can be the first pug to reach the Pole. They have some help from a Walrus, but when they arrive they see Granny Pug has got there first. Pug breaks another world record.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny accidentally drops his ice-cream, so Flop shares his fruit kebab

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Toodloo shows her balancing skills and Great BigHoo, while Chickedy and Chick run and roll out of the way

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin meets two Irish dancing stars who set him a challenge. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is trying to earn a star for his star chart by washing Lord Tumble's car

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: When the Teletubbies do the Tubby Phone Dance, the Noo-noo wants to join in

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with spoons and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels enjoy a day at the seaside when they are suddenly dwarfed by a giant, regal sailing ship that has ground onto the beach. The squirrels go to investigate, encountering penguins who explain they were on a very important mission when they got sick. They need to head to a nearby island but they are too poorly to sail the ship

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wingston's Daddy is running late so while all the other little monsters head home with their parents, a woeful Wingston helps Miss Grizzlesniff fill the goo pit

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. is in Craggy Sandy World. The Hovering Honk is hunting for turnips. B.O.T. struggles to keep out of the beastie’s way as it sucks up turnips, chomps them and honks! The turnips get bigger and bigger and so does the Honk when it eats them!

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Young animals must learn to hide. Andy Day sees how coatis follow mum's tail to safety in the forest. Eider ducks keep a wolf busy while their ducklings find a safe spot. The big mouthed hap is strangest of all. The young fish hide in its mouth

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: It is Sid's turn for a fun-filled adventure as he plays a show jumper and tries to win a competition by beating the favourite, Mary Ann Fetlock

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Olobobs find a pot in the forest and want to return it to its owner

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters arrive at the Bonneville Salt Flats in Utah, USA, a huge dry lakebed that is perfect for high-speed driving

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day heads 160 million years into the past in search of a feather belonging to a tiny prehistoric dinosaur, the epidexipteryx, but he is left stranded in the tree tops after encountering a meat-eating dinosaur

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle has so much fun on a doggy playdate at Mr Willow's house that he decides to stay for a sleepover. However, the Brooklyn-Bells miss Waffle and cannot wait for his return. With the voice of Rufus Hound

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Emily and Shelly start selling doughnuts and the holes in the snacks remind Jack of those that were once in a rainbow. Bernard Cribbins and Freema Agyeman star

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig loves playing subway trains. Woolly shows her how playing subway trains on a real subway is not a good idea

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘slippers’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. This episode is all about slippers – green slippers, blue slippers, little slippers and big slippers. We see prickly slippers and pom pom slippers, monster slippers and fairy slippers! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Birthday Girl is thrown an amazing party in an episode that also introduces the words stir, squirt, twirl and bird

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug go an adventure underground where they meet Granny Pug. They slide down a tunnel to the centre of the earth. When they arrive, they find some very friendly dinosaurs. Granny Pug promises to keep the dinosaur’s home a secret.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy has a pair of green socks waiting for him. He eats one, but loses the second and sets off to find it in Okido

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour gold, from treasure to golden sand, and explore how gold metal is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour gold.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go to the hairdressers and Mr Tumble pretends to be a hairdresser

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po enjoy songtime, singing solo and then singing together

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny sees Coco with a plaster on her finger and decides he wants one too - so makes one out of sticky tape

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in trouble when Glitch's feather collecting mission goes wrong.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Yabba and Timmy transform an old cardboard box into a fire engine, but end up causing more problems than they solve

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Rex learns that honesty is the best policy when he accidentally floods the pitch and Rachel challenges the team with some honest questions

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, Grace shows off three amazing machines that help with farming! Grace drives a massive compost turner, shows off a machine that makes hay bales and then picks some tasty carrots in a carrot harvester!

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line help a little sea monster collect donations of gold for a 'Save the Sea Monsters' charity

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly is digging in the garden when his spade suddenly hits a key. Duggee has his key badge, so they can find out through trial and error what it opens. The squirrels go on an adventure to work out what the key opens and what could be inside

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter uses a model owl to scare off Old Brown, but he ends up scaring the squirrels as well

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: A closer look at scuba diving gear. Mark tells us all about how his Scuba diving mask allows him to come face to face with amazing creatures under the sea, like jellyfish, dolphins and even sharks!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 106 millions years to the time of the muttaburasaurus - a 29ft-long dinosaur that lived in Australia and enjoyed eating plants and trees

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how train tracks work and visits a factory where coloured paint is made

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch's plans to help Tala discover facts about Africa go wrong when they are stranded in different parts of the continent. It's up to the Go Jetters to save them from the jungles of Congo and a hippo-infested river

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When Sam, David and Kobi find a lost piano, they aim to reunite it with the owner

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran put on a puppet show in the garden using odd socks!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady has run out of wool, so Sarah and Duck help her to harvest the weeping woollen tree

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After finding a paper cone useful for 'loud talking', Mr Onion arrives at a sleepy castle

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A space tortoise is mysteriously woken up by a tune Tiny first heard coming from the sky

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The friends ride around the garden on the Ninky Nonk. The Tombliboos and Makka Pakka wave to Upsy Daisy and Igglepiggle

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Isla Fisher reads A Big Day for Migs, by Jo Hodgkinson. It's a big day for Migs and he is feeling a little bit nervous, until he finds the perfect thing to help him overcome it

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people with interesting items to sell, including a doll designed to teach resuscitation, and a collection of dairy artefacts

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Elis James: Football Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian looks at how a turbulent period for Welsh football turned into one of international triumph. A long line of mangers had endured mixed results since the early Nineties. However, the investment in youth initiated by John Toshack and the good work of Gary Speed before his tragic death helped to lay the foundation for qualification for Euro 2016 and 2020

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Hampden Park. With commentary from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A wedding video cracks the case of a teenager's abduction and an expired pilot's licence exposes a fraudster flying under the radar. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Bear are at loggerheads, while Karen hopes for a relaxing day out with Rob. Ruhma finds a mother has disturbing symptoms following the birth of her baby

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne and his experts restore a Lloyd Loom-style chair, a pair of bookcases, and a pine table with matching chairs recovered from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in north Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart and Rainie head to the police station about Abi, while Karen and Tiffany accompany Bernie to her first weight-loss class. Mick is running late for Linda's scan, only for events to conspire against him - will he make it there on time? Lily is angry when Ruby betrays her trust, and Rocky tries his luck with Kathy once again

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick explains his absence to Linda and the couple discuss what's best for them and the baby. After telling Martin the bad news from the doctor, Ruby spends the evening with Lily and the pair begin to bond. Rocky steps in to help Kathy with the cafe, persuading her to finally take his number, and Tiffany feels left out as Keegan learns he has been shortlisted for the street food competition

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian finds it hard to keep his cool when Fletch blames him for Evie's disappearance. Sacha and Dom prepare to be interviewed for the clinical lead job. Will Sacha's experience win against Dom's ruthless ambition? Josh is determined to crack a mystery case, hoping to impress Ange and rekindle their relationship

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal-worker Dominic Chinea has a treat in store when he works on a rare bicycle sidecar that has not been used in decades, while furniture restorer Will Kirk needs all his skills when he is presented with a box of bits that used to be a Victorian sewing table, crushed to pieces in an accident. Can he piece this fragile 3D jigsaw back together? Percussion expert Pete Woods takes receipt of a military drum that saw frontline action in both world wars and horologist Steve Fletcher tries to get an art deco clock chiming again

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the opening matches in Group F - Hungary v Portugal at Puskas Arena in Budapest and France v Germany at Fußball Arena München. The match in Munich brought together the two most recent World Cup winners, and was also a rematch of their Euro 2016 semi-final, which the French won 2-0 in Marseille courtesy of two Antoine Griezmann goals. Germany coach Joachim Low will leave his post this summer after 15 years in charge - he oversaw the World Cup win in 2014 but couldn't prevent his side from suffering a shock early exit in 2018. Former England internationals Alex Scott and Rob Green provide analysis

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a European Championship-related countdown

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma's relationship with Romero takes a significant step, but Norman may not react well to the news. Meanwhile, Dylan tries to rid himself of old business. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman embarks on an impromptu adventure with a new friend, and while he's gone, Norma is given a taste of happiness at the White Pine Bay Festival. Romero faces an awkward situation and Dylan is caught between two families. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Germany. A chance to see both sides' opening Group F fixture, which took place at Fußball Arena München. With commentary from Jonathan Pearce

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits houses in Liverpool and Carlisle, and Dion Dublin is off to investigate a plot of land in Bury

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville ask who is responsible when valuables are stolen from a coach. Plus, the bride and groom left waiting at the airport

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A van's everyday satnav puts a pair of prolific animal thieves behind bars. An anonymous letter holds the key to cracking the identity of a blackmailer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at the Mona Showground on Anglesey, while host Anita Manning visits a jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Russia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group B fixture, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only met on four previous occasions, most recently in June 2009, when Russia recorded a straightforward 3-0 victory in a World Cup qualifier in Helsinki. Both sides will be delighted if they can secure three points here in their efforts to usurp group favourites Belgium and former winners Denmark, although the fact that three nations will progress from four of the six groups certainly improves their chances of reaching the last 16. Analysis is provided by Alex Scott and Micah Richards, while commentary comes from Robyn Cowen and Martin Keown

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the Group A encounter, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Current Wales manager Robert Page played in that match, and the former Watford defender will be hoping his back line will be more robust in this game. Page will also view this fixture as crucial to his side's chances of qualifying for the knockout stages once again, with the daunting prospect of a match against Italy in Rome still to come on Sunday. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. Callum feels helpless about Fitzy, so Whitney promises to help him, Suki gets on the wrong side of Vi, and Nancy and Frankie meddle in Shirley's love life. Jean has some advice for Ruby, who feels like giving up

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. Ben is suspicious when he spots Callum and Whitney getting into a cab together, Tiffany tries to cover her mistake and Rocky pulls out the stops to apologise to Sonia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make a little girl's bridesmaid dress, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodice and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Elis James and regular guest Danny Gabbidon look back at Wales' second match of the tournament against Turkey

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - Turkey v Wales and Italy v Switzerland in Group A, and Finland v Russia in Group B. Wales made the semi-finals of Euro 2016 despite losing their second group fixture to England. The Welsh had not beaten Turkey since 1981, while the last meeting - a World Cup qualifier in 1997 - resulted in a 6-4 win for the Turks in Istanbul

programme.title: Roberto Martinez: Whistle to Whistle
programme.desc: Cameras follow the Belgium national team manager Roberto Martinez as he prepares for Euro 2020. The documentary provides behind-the-scenes access to Martinez as he carries out his dual role as head coach and technical director of the number one ranked team in the world

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Bite-sized edition of the documentary, which sees Giles Coren and Monica Galetti working alongside the staff of Marina Bay Sands hotel in Singapore

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales. A chance to see the second Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Commentary from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: The plight of asylum seekers in Scotland has been described as a humanitarian crisis. Sam Poling meets men and women who fled here to escape danger, only to find themselves facing a bleak and uncertain future. She travels to the illegal migrant camps in France to speak to those prepared to risk their lives to get to the UK, and asks whether the current system for those seeking refuge is fit for purpose

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the opening matches in Group F - Hungary v Portugal at Puskas Arena in Budapest and France v Germany at Fußball Arena München. The match in Munich brought together the two most recent World Cup winners, and was also a rematch of their Euro 2016 semi-final, which the French won 2-0 in Marseille courtesy of two Antoine Griezmann goals. Germany coach Joachim Low will leave his post this summer after 15 years in charge - he oversaw the World Cup win in 2014 but couldn't prevent his side from suffering a shock early exit in 2018. Former England internationals Alex Scott and Rob Green provide analysis

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Russia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group B fixture, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only met on four previous occasions, most recently in June 2009, when Russia recorded a straightforward 3-0 victory in a World Cup qualifier in Helsinki. Both sides will be delighted if they can secure three points here in their efforts to usurp group favourites Belgium and former winners Denmark, although the fact that three nations will progress from four of the six groups certainly improves their chances of reaching the last 16. Analysis is provided by Alex Scott and Micah Richards, while commentary comes from Robyn Cowen and Martin Keown

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the Group A encounter, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Current Wales manager Robert Page played in that match, and the former Watford defender will be hoping his back line will be more robust in this game. Page will also view this fixture as crucial to his side's chances of qualifying for the knockout stages once again, with the daunting prospect of a match against Italy in Rome still to come on Sunday. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. Callum feels helpless about Fitzy, so Whitney promises to help him, Suki gets on the wrong side of Vi, and Nancy and Frankie meddle in Shirley's love life. Jean has some advice for Ruby, who feels like giving up

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. Ben is suspicious when he spots Callum and Whitney getting into a cab together, Tiffany tries to cover her mistake and Rocky pulls out the stops to apologise to Sonia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make a little girl's bridesmaid dress, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodice and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - Turkey v Wales and Italy v Switzerland in Group A, and Finland v Russia in Group B. Wales made the semi-finals of Euro 2016 despite losing their second group fixture to England. The Welsh had not beaten Turkey since 1981, while the last meeting - a World Cup qualifier in 1997 - resulted in a 6-4 win for the Turks in Istanbul

programme.title: Roberto Martinez: Whistle to Whistle
programme.desc: Cameras follow the Belgium national team manager Roberto Martinez as he prepares for Euro 2020. The documentary provides behind-the-scenes access to Martinez as he carries out his dual role as head coach and technical director of the number one ranked team in the world

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair, and finds out about a distinctive style of wood carving. Experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine assist the teams

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people with interesting items to sell, including a doll designed to teach resuscitation, and a collection of dairy artefacts

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the committee planning mid-Ulster's first Pride parade, which takes place in rural Northern Ireland's conservative and evangelical heartland. With the help of a local reverend, the organisers overcome opposition, setbacks, and a worldwide health pandemic to bring the event to life

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal-worker Dominic Chinea has a treat in store when he works on a rare bicycle sidecar that has not been used in decades, while furniture restorer Will Kirk needs all his skills when he is presented with a box of bits that used to be a Victorian sewing table, crushed to pieces in an accident. Can he piece this fragile 3D jigsaw back together? Percussion expert Pete Woods takes receipt of a military drum that saw frontline action in both world wars and horologist Steve Fletcher tries to get an art deco clock chiming again

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Libya helped arm the IRA, and for two decades victims have sought compensation from the African country. Reporter Stephen Dempster investigates the political obstacles thrown up in their path and why their pleas for Government intervention have not been heard

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
