Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Kingsman: The Secret Service (2015)
programme.desc: A streetwise teenager is taken under the wing of his dead father's old colleague, an agent working for a super-secret spy organisation. Will the youngster turn out to be a chip off the old block by proving useful during a battle against a twisted, hi-tech genius? Action comedy based on a comic book, starring Colin Firth, Taron Egerton and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Woman at War (2018)
programme.desc: An Icelandic environmental activist single-handedly fights a campaign of illegal sabotage against the aluminium industry. Her priorities shift when her application to adopt a Ukrainian orphan is accepted, leaving her struggling to balance the future of the Earth with the immediate needs of her child. Comedy drama, starring Halldóra Geirharðsdóttir and Jóhann Sigurðarson. In Icelandic, English, Ukrainian, Chinese and Spanish

programme.title: Night Moves (2013)
programme.desc: A farmer, a high-school drop-out and an ex-soldier form a militant environmentalist group, and plot to blow up a controversial dam. While their plan is successful, it has many shocking consequences, and they are faced with a series of moral dilemmas in the aftermath. Thriller, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Dakota Fanning and Peter Sarsgaard

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A war crimes investigator is on the trail of an infamous Nazi. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young and Edward G Robinson

programme.title: Blanche Fury (1948)
programme.desc: A penniless governess is invited to stay with a wealthy relative, where she hopes to secure her future and take over the estate by marrying her infatuated cousin. However, her secret romance with a stablehand has tragic consequences for all concerned. Drama, starring Valerie Hobson, Michael Gough, Stewart Granger and Walter Fitzgerald

programme.title: How Green Was My Valley (1941)
programme.desc: Residents of a Welsh mining community at the start of the 20th century struggle to come to terms with the decline in their traditional way of life. Oscar-winning drama, starring Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara, Donald Crisp and Roddy McDowall

programme.title: 12 Angry Men (1957)
programme.desc: Twelve jurors convene to decide the verdict in a murder trial. While 11 of the group are thoroughly convinced the teenage defendant is guilty, one lone voice of reason refuses to cave in to pressure and passionately argues the case for his innocence. Sidney Lumet's legal drama, starring Henry Fonda, Lee J Cobb, Ed Begley, Martin Balsam and Jack Klugman

programme.title: Fantastic Four (2015)
programme.desc: A gifted young scientist joins members of a secret project researching inter-dimensional travel, and they return from a journey to another world with superhuman powers. Their abilities make them the only hope for Earth's survival when a colleague who was lost on their expedition returns to wreak vengeance. Superhero adventure, starring Miles Teller, Michael B Jordan, Kate Mara and Jamie Bell

programme.title: Jack Reacher: Never Go Back (2016)
programme.desc: The nomadic vigilante tries to save an army major who has been framed for murder and treason. Jack Reacher discovers that Turner has been targeted by a secret government conspiracy, and together they go on the run to expose the truth. Director Edward Zwick's blockbusting action thriller sequel based on Lee Child's novel, starring Tom Cruise, Cobie Smulders, Danika Yarosh and Aldis Hodge

programme.title: The Festival (2018)
programme.desc: When Nick's girlfriend dumps him at graduation, he has a colossal meltdown in front of the entire university. He's convinced his life is over, but his best mate Shane has the perfect solution - three days at a music festival. With the help of 'festival aficionado' and certified oddball Amy, Shane tries to get Nick to embrace the music, the mayhem and the mud. Comedy, starring Joe Thomas, Hannah Tointon, Hammed Animashaun and Jemaine Clement

programme.title: Brick Lane (2007)
programme.desc: A Bangladeshi woman reluctantly leaves her native country for an arranged marriage in England. Years spent living in a drab east-London tower block take their toll, while her husband is consumed by his thwarted ambitions. However, an encounter with a cocky younger man changes her outlook on life. Drama, based on Monica Ali's novel, starring Tannishtha Chatterjee and Satish Kaushik

programme.title: My Man Godfrey (1936)
programme.desc: A spoiled aristocratic woman hires a tramp as her butler, little anticipating how valuable he will prove _ or what effect his charms will have on her. Classic comedy, starring William Powell, Carole Lombard and Alice Brady

programme.title: Rawhide (1951)
programme.desc: Outlaws hold staff and a passenger and her baby hostage at a remote stagecoach station while awaiting the arrival of a shipment of $100,000 in gold bullion. The nervous captives try to stay calm, but the tension between the gang leader and a jumpy henchman grows fraught, ending in a frantic shoot-out - with the infant in the thick of the fray. Western, starring Tyrone Power, Susan Hayward and Jack Elam

programme.title: Red Mountain (1951)
programme.desc: A Confederate captain sets out to join forces with notorious guerilla militia Quantrill's Raiders, but stops off en route to rescue a man from being lynched for a murder he himself committed - and complicates matters by falling for his fiancee. Western set during the American Civil War, starring Alan Ladd and Lizabeth Scott

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King (1975)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine

programme.title: The Day the Earth Stood Still (2008)
programme.desc: An alien arrives on Earth, accompanied by an awesomely powerful robot, on a fateful mission - to decide if the human race deserves to continue - and if not, to wipe it out. The visitor is imprisoned in a military facility, but a scientist helps him escape, hoping to prove that mankind is worth saving. Sci-fi drama remake, starring Keanu Reeves and Jennifer Connelly

programme.title: Hancock (2008)
programme.desc: A reluctant, alcoholic superhero ends up the most hated man in Los Angeles thanks to his habit of causing mayhem and destruction while trying to do good. However, when he saves the life of a PR guru, the grateful executive sets about transforming the public perception of the crime-fighter, despite his wife's strong misgivings. Comedy, starring Will Smith, Jason Bateman and Charlize Theron

programme.title: Hunt for the Wilderpeople (2016)
programme.desc: A 12-year-old brought up in the city is sent to be fostered by the good-natured Bella and her grumpy husband Hec, who live on a farm in rural New Zealand. When tragedy strikes. Ricky runs off into the wilderness, pursued by Hec - inadvertently sparking a national manhunt for the two of them. Comedy adventure written and directed by Taika Waititi, starring Sam Neill and Julian Dennison

programme.title: Child 44 (2015)
programme.desc: A secret police agent in 1950s Russia is disgraced for refusing to denounce his own wife as a traitor. Reassigned to a remote outpost, he tries to bring a serial child murderer to justice, but faces an official cover-up by a rival, who wants to maintain the illusion that Soviet society is without crime. Detective thriller based on Tom Rob Smith's novel, starring Tom Hardy, Gary Oldman and Noomi Rapace

programme.desc: Mel and Joe are given a taste of the future when they are frozen during a freak avalanche, and thawed out 100 years later, only to discover the world has changed dramatically during the course of the last century. Comedy, directed by and starring Joey Lawrence, with Melissa Joan Hart

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to relax after he is forced to take a holiday, and when Penny quits her job the duo end up spending time together. Meanwhile, Leonard struggles to be supportive of his girlfriend's career decision, Bernadette seeks Stuart's help in replacing one of Howard's comic books, and Howard and Raj provide Amy with some advice. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When the guys are unable to get tickets for Comic-Con, Leonard, Howard and Raj decide to buy fake passes from a stranger - but they then worry about getting caught with them. Sheldon decides to hold his own comic-book convention and searches for a suitable star guest for the event. Meanwhile, the girls take tea at a hotel but discover that maturity is over-rated. Comedy, guest starring James Earl Jones and Carrie Fisher

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray gets his moment in the spotlight when he is honoured by his old high school for his basketball prowess. While he and Beverly are away from home, Barry takes advantage of their absence to throw a party to impress his school friends

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tries to express his feelings for Dana by making her a mix tape, but disaster strikes when the tape falls into Beverly's hands. Barry obtains a fake ID and takes it to school to show off to his classmates. David Spade (Rules of Engagement) guest stars, with Sean Giambrone, Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: A new captain at the Nine-Nine attempts to ingratiate herself with Jake and the squad by inviting them over to her house for a dinner party

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard and Bernadette decide they do not want to know the gender of their unborn child, but are upset when they hear Raj has found out before they have. After Amy's apartment floods, she proposes a cohabitation experiment with Sheldon to stay with him while her apartment undergoes repairs

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Things don't run smoothly as Amy and Sheldon's cohabitation experiment continues, forcing Leonard and Penny to intervene when the couple have an argument about the 'bathroom schedule'. Howard and Bernadette cancel their holiday plans when Bernadette feels unwell and decide to have a secret stay-cation at home instead. However, the pair are soon disturbed by unexpected guests dropping by

Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

Sheldon decides to hold his own comic-book convention and searches for a suitable star guest for the event. Meanwhile, the girls take tea at a hotel but discover that maturity is over-rated. Comedy, guest starring James Earl Jones and Carrie Fisher

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray orders the cluttered garage be cleared out, although he insists on keeping one trunk that he is particularly sentimental about. Adam and Barry tease Erica mercilessly after discovering her old collection of New Kids on the Block memorabilia

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As the school dance approaches, Erica asks Beverly to help her find Barry a date to keep him away from Lainey, but Beverly comes up with a candidate who surprises everyone. Meanwhile, Adam and Pops get into a huge argument. Allie Grant (Suburgatory) guest stars

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Andre Jr is accepted by both Howard and Stanford universities, Dre tries to convince him to attend his alma mater. Jack develops a comedy style and Ruby decides she's going to manage his career, forcing Diane to compete for her attention

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: The family is divided over the idea of getting a dog after Dre and Rainbow promise Jack he can have one if he achieves straight As at school. Rainbow's brother Johan is back in town but he doesn't get the warm welcome he was expecting from Ruby

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The entire squad is on high alert when a massive blackout hits Brooklyn. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg as a detective with a light-hearted approach to crime

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Detective Jake Peralta and his eclectic group of colleagues at Brooklyn's 99th Precinct are used to having a commanding officer who doesn't make them work too hard or follow the rules too closely. That all changes when tightly wound Captain Ray Holt takes over, determined to make the dysfunctional squad into the best in the borough. Pilot episode of the American comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tests a newly invented device after hearing that it can help him to read people's emotions. Leonard isn't happy when he finds out that Penny's brother is coming to town so that she can help him to find a job. Raj gathers his ex-girlfriends together so that they can give him some dating advice

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard attempt to distract Sheldon with a trip to a historic railway when he slows the progress of their work on the Air Force guidance system. Raj and Stuart look after baby Halley, while Penny and Amy take Bernadette out

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beautician Claire hosts the third dinner party in Cardiff, but her decision to serve her favourite dishes on planks instead of plates proves controversial

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows. programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by American Murder: The Family Next Door, Simply Nigella, The Andrew Marr Show's interview with Boris Johnson, Top Gear, The Savoy, Surgeons: At the Edge of Life, The Yorkshire Vet, and BBC and ITV News coverage of President Trump and coronavirus. First aired in October

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny discovers she has a dedicated group of fans from her Serial Ape-ist days when she accompanies Leonard to a comic-con. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Amy host a brunch at Penny's flat, and stress over the baby's arrival prompts Howard to make an impulsive purchase

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After the repairs are completed in Amy's flat, she lies to Sheldon by telling him that the work is still ongoing so they can carry on living together for a while longer. Leonard is upset when he finds out Penny has been moving his collectibles out of Sheldon's old room and into storage behind his back

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The nation's favourite armchair critics share their opinions on shows airing in April 2016, including Guy Martin's Wall of Death: Live, Bake Off Creme de la Creme, EastEnders, Bear Grylls: Mission Survive, Antiques Roadshow, This Farming Life, The Night Manager and the news. The programme captures their instant reactions and lively - sometimes emotional - discussions from the comfort of their own homes

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Edinburgh musician Jamie says he is fed up of attracting groupies and wants more than a brief fling. Twenty-year-old musical theatre student Jasmin from Bognor Regis has been diagnosed with the chronic fatigue illness ME and wants to meet someone who will accept her for who she is. Anna Richardson invites them both to choose a date from a selection of six naked people, whose bodies are revealed one section at a time

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jade, 24, sits down for a meal with Jak, 29, a yoga lover in touch with his spiritual side and who shares her love of exercise and tattoos, while fashion stylist Christine, 70, and entrepreneur Peter, 71, share stories of the Swinging Sixties. Health club manager Donna, 51, is a born romantic with old fashioned values at heart and her date is 53-year-old stetson-wearing Simon from Dallas, Texas, who's happy to stand out from the crowd and is looking for a strong woman to stand by his side

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows. This week they consider The Circle, CBeebies Bedtime Story, Escape from Pretoria, Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourite, Baywatch, This Morning and Naked Attraction

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben enlists Riley's help to prove to the bar's owner that he is responsible enough to fill the new manager's job, but his tactic of posing as a family man soon backfires. Bonnie buys baby Emma a very girly pushchair, but Tucker and Danny exchange it for one they believe to be much cooler in the hope of attracting the attention of women

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley stands in as captain of softball league team after they keep losing, but her competitive side soon takes over and she starts taking the role too seriously, while Danny finds out that Bonnie is dating his hockey coach

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Dr Cox is overwhelmed by his new role as Chief of Medicine, and reaches out to Dr Kelso for friendship and support. Comedy, starring John C McGinley, Zach Braff and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Ted falls in love with a ukulele player, and Dr Cox realises he cannot handle everything that is thrown at him. Comedy, starring Zach Braff and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Devon County Show, where she discovers the secret of home-made handscrub and learns about beekeeping and making beeswax candles. She also tries her hand at crafting jewellery in silver clay - an ideal way of making unusual but inexpensive gifts

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Dave and Debbie Chambers from Redditch, Worcestershire, started their family young, but now their four children have grown up, they've decided to recapture their youth and buy a place in Spain where they can enjoy time in the sun - just the two of them. For their budget of £45,000 Laura Hamilton shows them five apartments in and around the town of Torrevieja, but will any of them be their perfect place?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien hope a clay pigeon trap, a searchlight and a Peugeot 205 will help make a profit for their owners

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A simple case becomes complicated when family secrets are uncovered, while another reveals a great-grandfather's life on the docks in the early days of diving. An uncle's part in the defeat of Nazi Germany in the Second World War is also highlighted. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be traced

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A straight-talking hairdresser from London tries to cut it as Costa Blanca's newest stylist and newlyweds start a new life as holiday hosts in the south of France

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Unicorn Inn in Ludlow, Shropshire, where husband and wife team Lee and Zoe Osbourne hope their glamorous fairy-tale style rooms will impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the East Walls Hotel in Chichester, West Sussex, owned by history-loving husband and wife Celia and Damon Haughey, who have rejected the boutique label

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Wheatsheaf in Sandbach, Cheshire, where owner Jake Pare and general manager Chris Baldwin hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is at the Shawries Hotel in Blackpool, where the dated decor takes two guests back to their childhood and breakfast is a build-your-own sandwich platter

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. East Walls Hotel is first up, and owners Celia and Damon need to clean up Dave's toilet brush troubles before broaching his beef with the breakfast banger

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: The daughter of a world-famous motorbike inventor takes on the multi-million-dollar task of restoring the eclectic earthquake-damaged family home in Christchurch

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Dagmar the giraffe is supporting her closest herd member Orla, whose new calf is overdue - but at the same time she is reluctant to allow her own youngest to feed properly. So the keepers plan a herd birth in the hope that both mothers can learn from each other. There is concern when two of the spider monkeys withdraw from their family, while two devoted camels experience the trauma of separation when one undergoes an operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul move into their new home in western France, but struggle with the language, while Adam from Warwickshire is hoping to join the band of expat chateau owners

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to north Cornwall to see one of his favourite Grand Designs, and discover whether Rebecca Sturrock and Gregory Kewish were successful with their ambitious plans. Their project, to reinforce the walls of an old bungalow and put a new living space on top made entirely of cross-laminated timber, soon ran into difficulties, but now fully complete both inside and out, Kevin finds out whether this innovative house is really big enough to accommodate a growing family

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: A visual journey around Snowdonia, starting at the coastal town of Harlech and taking in the Italianate village of Portmeirion, Bala Lake, and the slate mine of Dinorwic

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Featuring people who have faced challenges and transformed their lives, including Nicole, who burnt her foot with caustic soda while cleaning her drains. Nicole reveals that she was born a man, and while growing up in Scarborough, she dreamed of becoming a pop star. Cameras also focus on Francis, 28, who has fallen off his bike and is suffering from severe amnesia

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams compete against Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson. David Baddiel joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: A visual journey around Snowdonia, starting at the coastal town of Harlech and taking in the Italianate village of Portmeirion, Bala Lake, and the slate mine of Dinorwic

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp plans to surprise her guests with two main courses - roast beef with mulled wine gravy, and masala cauliflower with coconut stuffing for the vegetarians

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Keen walkers Rosie and Michael Mulroy from Durham are looking for a holiday home from which they can explore the stunning Andalusian hills and countryside around Frigiliana in southern Spain. Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for their £120,000 budget, from town centre apartments to quaint village townhouses, but will any of them appeal to both heart and head?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Research into two family trees uncovers how Britain's post-war building boom boosted Irish immigration, and how the British infantry fought their way across the border into Nazi Germany. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs are still being sought

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A London hairdresser takes on her first member of staff, and the pressure is on for a retired major and his wife as they become holiday hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the White Swan in Deighton, North Yorkshire, where Roman Solnar and Tamsyn Muller hope their guests will appreciate their no-nonsense approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Clough View Farm in Matlock, Derbyshire, where hosts Lisa Hardy and Sally Dakin hope that their generosity of spirit will be a hit with their guests. Nick and David count the costs and serve up some suspicions at dinner. A long wait for breakfast leaves the guests grumbling, and Tamsyn and Roman find their feedback hard to stomach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Buxted Inn in Uckfield, East Sussex, run by Nick Bolton and chef David Kirby, and where the focus is on food and wine. Lisa is charmed by the character of the place, but Farid and John think it has a corporate feel. At breakfast, a menu misunderstanding leaves Farid with more than he bargained for

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Crescent Victoria hotel in Margate, Kent, where hosts Farid Al-Faisal and John Dennis aim to treat their guests like royalty. Unfortunately, only one of the pairs feel like princesses, with the rest being less than impressed by the attempt at luxury

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The White Swan is first and Roman and Tamsyn have some cracks to paper over from previous critiques in the week

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Antiques-lover Patty scours the country looking for historic materials to use in the building of her new build in Mangawhai - including a 100-year-old wharf

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Keepers introduce a new male to the spotty chocolate poison dart frog colony, hoping that the competition will result in an increase in tadpoles. Meanwhile, the Sulawesi macaques are ecstatic to welcome a new baby to their family, but their enthusiasm is a cause for concern as new mother Maggie may not be able to protect her baby from the other females

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul start transforming their abandoned scullery into a kitchen. Meanwhile, Adam goes for a second viewing at a property on the slopes of the Pyrenees

programme.title: Grand Designs: Living in
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud presents one of four special programmes inspired by 15 years of Grand Designs. He begins with a look at the challenges of self-building in the city, where space is at a premium and architects have to be at their most ingenious. Kevin uses footage from the best urban builds from the show over the years, meets pioneers at the forefront of the latest architectural thinking and looks at clever innovations that continue to transform people's lives to reveal which designs work best and why

programme.title: Royals Declassified
programme.desc: The story of Anthony Blunt, royal appointed art expert and KGB spy, who worked as Surveyor of the Queen's Pictures for 27 years. The programme examines how he was previously employed by George VI to loot art treasures from Germany in the aftermath of the Second World War, and that despite Blunt's protestations of loyalty to the monarchy, classified documents have since revealed that he provided damaging information about the royal family to his Soviet employers

programme.title: Spying on the Royals
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary examining an espionage operation conducted to gather information on Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson. Shown to mark the 80th anniversary of the king's abdication, the programmes examines recently declassified documents revealing the extent of the surveillance and who ordered it, as well as featuring dramatic reconstructions and eyewitness accounts

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow 56-year-old Dave, who is brought to St George's after falling from a ladder at work. Doctors are concerned that he may have life-changing damage to his spine. Meanwhile, three-year-old Alf arrives in resus with his parents after being hit by a car

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Royals Declassified
programme.desc: The story of Anthony Blunt, royal appointed art expert and KGB spy, who worked as Surveyor of the Queen's Pictures for 27 years. The programme examines how he was previously employed by George VI to loot art treasures from Germany in the aftermath of the Second World War, and that despite Blunt's protestations of loyalty to the monarchy, classified documents have since revealed that he provided damaging information about the royal family to his Soviet employers

programme.title: Spying on the Royals
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary examining an espionage operation conducted to gather information on Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson. Shown to mark the 80th anniversary of the king's abdication, the programmes examines recently declassified documents revealing the extent of the surveillance and who ordered it, as well as featuring dramatic reconstructions and eyewitness accounts

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team members select some of their favourite investigations from the show. Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to learn about the difference between black and white pepper, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves, while Matt Tebbutt is in Switzerland to find out why there are holes in Swiss cheese

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Roger and Obie Pearce want a slice of the Spanish good life. Obie has never been to Spain before, and Roger hasn't visited since he was in the army 40 years ago, but they are determined to relocate to Almeria where Roger's health could benefit from the warm climate. Ben Hillman sets out to find them their perfect home on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Research into family trees uncovers the history of domestic service in one of the world's most prestigious universities and how luxury transatlantic steamliners helped change the course of history. Plus, details of unclaimed estates with heirs still needing to be found

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A hairdresser has grand plans to expand in the Costa Blanca, and a couple's dream of a new life in France running a yoga retreat falls at the first hurdle

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Just B in Edinburgh, owned by Karen MacDonald and Geoff Thomson, who hope that their slice of Zen will impress their guests. Nick and Stefan are forced to comply with the house rules in some alternative attire, while Tony and Natalie make some dirty discoveries in their dog-friendly room. At dinner, the hosts serve up their spiritual side, and the competition hots up as Stefan reveals his hospitality pedigree. In the morning, a mushroom misdemeanour leaves a bad taste in Tony's mouth

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Glencoe Villa Guesthouse in Featherstone, West Yorkshire, owned by Ewa Hunt, who is joined by her best friend, Ellie. The homely styled bedrooms aren't to everyone's taste, and at dinner there's a debate about how to host. At breakfast, some guests pick holes in the pre-order system, and in the kitchen Ewa's under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Fox Inn in Stourton, Staffordshire, owned by uncle and nephew Stefan Carron and Nick Edwards. Most of their guests are bowled over by the bedrooms, but Tony isn't wild about the decor. At dinner, Stefan impresses with some serious celebrity name dropping and Tony reveals his alternative approach to taking on staff. At breakfast, pro chef Nick pulls out all the stops with his presentation, but missing items mean feedback falls short from Tony

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The New Inn in Newton, Derbyshire, owned by Tony Carlin, who's joined by his partner Natalie Briggs. On first impressions the guests feel it's lacking luxury, and Ewa and Ellie have cleanliness concerns. At dinner, the luxury label is called into question, and a bust up over the self-service breakfast is narrowly avoided. At breakfast time, the hosts do a disappearing act, which doesn't go down well with their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Just B is first, and owners Karen and Geoff get some answers while hearing the truth from guests on their hair-raising stay

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Ric and Boio set out to turn a childhood treehouse dream into reality. But building in a pine forest, which is frequently prone to winter storms, only complicates things

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Keepers introduce elderly chameleon Mr Parsons to two new prospective partners, and bongo Malaeka shows little interest in her potential mate, Moti. Meanwhile in the aquarium, George the pinstripe damba is spotted in distress, and keepers notice a lump on his gill which may be affecting his breathing

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge takes children Arthur and Dorothy on a mission to rescue baby toads in their chateau grounds. Donna and Paul put the rest of their renovations on hold as they set themselves the challenge of turning a wing of the chateau into a self-contained apartment for Donna's parents, Anne and Brian

programme.title: Grand Designs: Living in
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud looks at the challenges of self-building in the UK's most remote places, where beautiful landscapes often meet savage weather. Drawing on footage from 15 years of the programme, as well as new visits to extraordinary and incredible homes, Kevin reveals which designs work best, and why. Along the way, he interviews pioneers at the forefront of architecture in the wilderness and sees the astonishing lengths people go to live on the edges of society

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Lloyd and Caroline, who plan to build a sustainable home in Leicestershire. Charlie is worried that their eco-design might cost them the earth, but former soldier Lloyd is determined to run the build like a military operation. However, even his organisational skills cannot ward off a huge crisis with their budget, and what starts out as a passion project soon turns into an endurance test

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Marc and Felicity spend years building their dream non-toxic home in Melbourne, only for the unthinkable to happen when fire strikes just before they are about to move in. Will their hopes be shattered for ever?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. Doctors are concerned that he may have serious injuries to his pelvis and spine. There's also a profile of four-year-old Jeremy, who is struggling to breathe. He suffers from a series of chronic conditions, including microcephaly and epilepsy

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and Michelle Wolf take on guest captain Jonathan Ross with Johnny Vegas. Three-man comedy act Pappy's joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Building the Dream

However, even his organisational skills cannot ward off a huge crisis with their budget, and what starts out as a passion project soon turns into an endurance test

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the team's favourite investigations. Kate Quilton learns how a special enzyme contained in pineapples attacks and destroys dead skin cells in the mouth, with scientists using this enzyme to treat the victims of burns. Jimmy Doherty investigates the catastrophic effects of not getting enough vitamin D when he meets a boy who has rickets, while Matt Tebbutt finds out how a Lincolnshire business produces 1.5 million bottles of elderflower cordial without employing a single flower-picker

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart

He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. This edition also features a track test of the Danish Zenvo ST1 supercar and James Blunt returns for another drive in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Examining the 1966 murders of three police officers in Shepherd's Bush that led to a huge manhunt and calls for the death penalty to be reinstated

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Part one of two. A re-examination of Jack the Ripper's crimes, revealing how the story known today was shaped by the sensationalist press of 1888. This documentary strips back decades of rumour and misinformation to reveal the true lives of the five women slain

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates what Hitler intended to do with the priceless works of art stolen by the Nazis from across Europe, as well as his obsession with religious artefacts

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of London's Crossrail network, a project of staggering proportions, with 10,000 personnel working across 40 sites and on 118km of track. The focus should have been on the impressive engineering feat, though delays and budgets have become the focus of attention

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Glasgow to Cumbrae, learning about a forgotten Scottish expedition to the Antarctic and how one woman led a protest against high rents in the city

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgow's Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michael's final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, where items include a sapphire ring and a sketch by Robert Baden-Powell, founder of the Boy Scouts and Girl Guides. Glass expert Andy McConnell challenges the owner of a collection of mid-18th century drinking glasses to identify the lone fake, while an intricate model of a butcher's shop intrigues Fergus Gambon

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Bruce Parkin steps off a train with his family to attend his retirement party after 52 years' service, making him one of Europe's longest-serving drivers. The redevelopment of Reading station is the largest overhaul on the Great Western network since the days of Brunel, and Murray Parkin and his team carry out the last engine change at the historic Cow Lane site before they head over to their new state-of-the-art terminal

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of the island in the Indian Ocean, off the southeastern coast of Africa. It has an ecosystem that has stood in isolation for millions of years, and produced an array of wildlife that cannot be found anywhere else on the planet. He begins by observing lemurs in their habitat, male red giraffe-necked weevils fighting each other, the courting rituals of chameleons, and the behaviour of spiders and fossas

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragon's teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Hermann Goering - the dashing, young, down-on-his-luck war veteran who turned into an extravagant, heartless, obese, drug-addled Nazi billionaire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, where items include a sapphire ring and a sketch by Robert Baden-Powell, founder of the Boy Scouts and Girl Guides. Glass expert Andy McConnell challenges the owner of a collection of mid-18th century drinking glasses to identify the lone fake, while an intricate model of a butcher's shop intrigues Fergus Gambon

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex, beginning by travelling from Warwick to Radley in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers, and in Cliveden explores a great Italianate mansion

programme.title: WW2 - Frontlines
programme.desc: Midway became the location for the most decisive naval battle in the Pacific War. The Japanese should have triumphed, yet the Americans turned the Japanese trap into a bold ambush

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Guided missiles, the Wasserfall, and the war-changing V2 were dreamed up and tested in Peenemunde, a German seaside city suddenly overtaken by Nazi elite

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A highway built in the Brazilian jungle as an escape route in the event of an emergency at a nuclear power station, a Soviet fortress and Ellis Island hospital

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The gun battle on the thoroughfares of London in 1911 known as the Siege of Sidney Street. Four hundred rounds of ammunition were fired over six hours in one of the earliest news incidents to be captured by motion camera

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston uncovers the truth behind James Bond, revealing what elements of the character were drawn from creator Ian Fleming's own experiences

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of the Tokyo Skytree, the world's tallest free-standing tower, which uses liquid springs and safety glass to withstand earthquakes and high winds

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A fully relocatable polar research station in Antarctica, which was inspired by a revolutionary icebreaking ship and ingenious designs for pre-fabricated buildings

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex, beginning by travelling from Warwick to Radley in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers, and in Cliveden explores a great Italianate mansion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits Leicester's Museum of Science and Technology at Abbey Pumping Station, which provides a unique backdrop for the programme. Mark Hill casts his expert eye over an abandoned sculpture that looks strangely familiar - could it be a lost masterpiece? Richard Price values a clock powered by gravity and John Axford explains how a tiny frog can have a big price tag

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: A major service disruption hits Paddington on Meet the Manager day, and there is a look at Brunel's last engineering masterpiece, the Royal Albert Bridge, which spans the River Tamar and connects Devon and Cornwall. A fitter has to repair a faulty peak-time train full of passengers, and Charlbury station prepares for the Wilderness festival

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: An observation of the wildlife that inhabits the narrow band of mountains and rainforest on the island's east coast, from ring-tailed lemurs living on an inhospitable peak, to forest-dwelling sifakas, small primates on the verge of extinction. The programme also focuses on the golden bamboo lemur, which is capable of consuming daily doses of cyanide, and looks at the fruit-rich treetops where red-ruffed lemurs defend their food and territory. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: The impact of an integrated defence system and radar on the Second World War, focusing on the role of RAF Kenley, one of the most intact Battle of Britain airfields

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: Guy Walters explores Hitler's plans for the future after the Second World War, beginning by revealing what Britain under Nazi occupation would have looked like

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook, Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the country to discover how the railways have affected people and communities, and the legacy they have left behind. He begins in Manchester, where he finds out how the world's first industrialised city produced a revolutionary political movement, and learns about the railway workers who founded one of the most successful football clubs of all time. Along the way, the presenter does the washing in Port Sunlight - a model village on the Wirral - and hears stories about the aptly named George Francis Train's time in Birkenhead, Merseyside

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey around north-west England in the elegant resort town of Southport, where the railways provided thousands of holidaymakers with the chance enjoy the fun of the fair. On his journey around some of the region's larger towns, the presenter uncovers the history of Victorian entrepreneurship in Wigan and the beginnings of the Industrial Revolution in Bolton, before heading to smaller Leyland to get behind the wheel of a 100-year-old commercial vehicle

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off a Jensen Interceptor and a Jensen Healey along with a one-off Wood & Pickett Triumph Dolomite Sprint. Sarah takes a shine to a four-tonne road roller

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1971 Mercedes SL and a 1989 Jaguar XJS emerge from a barn on a chicken farm, a Roller and a Rover go under the hammer and a 1966 Triumph Spitfire is restored to near perfection

programme.desc: Herbert Rowse Armstrong, who was convicted of murdering his wife with arsenic. Much of the evidence against him was circumstantial and the scientific testimony remains disputed. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits the Parliament Buildings at Stormont Estate in Belfast, which has played a huge role in Northern Ireland's political history. An unloved muffin dish holds a surprise and Justin Croft spots an unread copy of Ulysses by James Joyce. John Foster delves underground into Belfast's 18th-century water system with an old wooden water pipe, while Adam Schoon meets a man with a phenomenal George Best collection

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Reigate Fort, which was repurposed to block the Nazis from getting to London and was set to become Resistance HQ should the capital have fallen

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
